titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/286

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=740 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:27:06.488381+00 | translation moa=741 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:27:03.845592+00

p.cl: Omnibus sancte Matris ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris Willelmus de Vesci salutem .

me concessisse et hac mea carta confirmasse . diuine pietatis intuitu et pro salute mea et pro salute omnium
antecessorum et successorum meorum deo et ecclesie sancte Marie de Melros et monachis ibidem deo seruienti
bus . illas duas carucatas terre . quarum unam dedit eis Patricius de Ridale . et aliam Walterus
filius eius . In territorio de Lillescliue . Cum pasturis et pertinentiis suis . per easdem diuisas que con
tinentur . In cartis predictorum patricii et Walteri filii eius . Et uolo et concedo ut predicti monachi . predicas ter
ras et pasturas habeant et teneant . In liberam puram et perpetuam elemosinam . Cum omnibus ai
siamentis suis et libertatibus . ita Libere . quiete . plene et honorifice  sicut aliquam elemosinam . Liberi
us . quietius . plenius et honorificentius In regno scotie tenent et possident . Saluo mihi et heredi
bus meis seruicio ad ipsas terras pertinente de prefatis patricio et Waltero et eorum heredibus . Ego etiam
et heredes mei Manutenebimus et warantizabimus predicta omnia predictis monachis . vt ea habe
ant et teneant et utantur modis omnibus quibus eis melius et utlius uisum fuerit  sicut predicto
rum donatorum carte et confirmationes Margerie matris mee quas inde habent predicti monachi tes
tantur .

Hiis Testibus . Domino . I . de Mackuswel . Rob’ de Ros . Waldeuo Marscallo . Willelmo Latimer
Rad’ Bardolf . Simone de horsley . Rob’ de Heuurth’ et Multis Aliis
To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and future William de Vescy sends greeting.

to have granted and by this my charter to have confirmed, moved by love of God and for the salvation of my soul and for the salvation of all my ancestors and successors, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks in that place serving God, the two ploughgates of land, one of which Patrick of Ryedale gave to them, and the other Walter his son, in the territory of Lilliesleaf, with the pastures belonging to them, by the same boundaries which are contained in the charters of the aforesaid Patrick and Walter his son. And I will and grant that the aforesaid monks have and hold the aforesaid lands and pastures in free, pure and perpetual alms, with all their easements and liberties, as freely, quitely, fully and honourably as they hold and possess any alms freely, quitely, fully and honourably in the kingdom of Scotland, reserving to myself and my heirs the service pertaining to those lands from the aforesaid Patrick and Walter and their heirs. I and my heirs also maintain and warrant all things aforesaid to the aforesaid monks, that they have and hold and use them by all means which seem best and useful to them, just as the charters of the aforesaid donors and confirmations of Margery my mother which the aforesaid monks have thereupon bear witness.
With these witnesses, the lord J(ohn) of Maxwell, Robert of Roos, Waldef the marischal, William the latimer (interpreter), Ralph Bardolf, Simon of Horsley, Robert of Airth, and many others.
